# Quirgs

> Governance-first AI skills for Claude Code. Quirgs publishes a catalog of installable Skills (governance SOPs an agent can run), Guides, and a human-in-the-loop approval Gate. Governance first. Build accordingly.

Quirgs is a governance-first AI product platform. A **quirg** is a unit of agent-legible governance — distributed in three formats: **Skill**, **Guide**, and **Tool**.

Skills install into Claude Code via the plugin marketplace and run as structured procedures. Every skill's source is viewable as a public GitHub Gist before install, and auto-update is off by default. The skills are governance *scaffolding* for education and drafting — they are not legal advice or a formal compliance determination.

Install any skill in two commands:

```
/plugin marketplace add unqdlphn/quirgs
/plugin install <skill-name>@quirgs
```

## Compliance bundle (Skills)

- [EU AI Act Classifier](https://quirgs.com/skills/eu-ai-act-classifier/): Classify an AI system's risk tier under the EU AI Act and output a role-scoped obligations checklist.
- [HITL Compliance Gate](https://quirgs.com/skills/hitl-compliance-gate/): Insert a structured human-review checkpoint into any AI workflow (EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001).
- [NIST AI RMF Checkpoint](https://quirgs.com/skills/nist-ai-rmf-checkpoint/): Walk the Govern / Map / Measure / Manage functions and produce a stage-scoped risk profile.
- [ISO 42001 Audit Prep](https://quirgs.com/skills/iso-42001-audit-prep/): Clause-by-clause ISO/IEC 42001:2023 AI Management System audit-readiness and gap analysis.
- [PII Exposure Checker](https://quirgs.com/skills/pii-exposure-checker/): Audit AI data pipelines for PII exposure under GDPR and EU AI Act Article 10.
- [AI Transparency Writer](https://quirgs.com/skills/ai-transparency-writer/): Draft transparency notices, model cards, and Article 13 system disclosures.
- [Incident Response Logger](https://quirgs.com/skills/incident-response-logger/): Structure AI incident reports aligned to NIST SP 800-61 and EU AI Act Article 62.

## Publish bundle (Skills)

- [Publish Workflow](https://quirgs.com/skills/publish-workflow/): Weekly publishing OS — routes tasks to the right skill, in the right order.
- [Publish Provenance](https://quirgs.com/skills/publish-provenance/): Creation log and AI-involvement assessment for every release.
- [Publish Update](https://quirgs.com/skills/publish-update/): Catalog metadata hygiene — find gaps, fix errors, enrich records.
- [Publish Broadcast](https://quirgs.com/skills/publish-broadcast/): DDEX ERN + PRO registration packets, ready for submission.
- [Publish License](https://quirgs.com/skills/publish-license/): Three-agent sync pipeline — brief to pitch in one pass.
- [Publish Income](https://quirgs.com/skills/publish-income/): Reconcile royalty statements against your catalog ledger.
- [Publish Shield](https://quirgs.com/skills/publish-shield/): Pre-release platform compliance + AI governance policy for music publishers.
- [Publish Harvest](https://quirgs.com/skills/publish-harvest/): Quarterly catalog performance review — tiers, gaps, and next actions.

## Platform

- [Skills registry](https://quirgs.com/skills/): Full catalog with status, frameworks, and per-skill install commands.
- [Bundles](https://quirgs.com/bundles/): Skill bundles grouped by use case.
- [HITL Gate](https://quirgs.com/hitl/): Human-in-the-loop approval queue that sits between an AI agent and any action requiring sign-off. A pull request review, but for AI decisions. Includes curl examples to post and approve an event.
- [Guides](https://quirgs.com/guides/): Long-form references on agent skills and governance workflow.

## Content provenance

The compliance skills (eu-ai-act-classifier, nist-ai-rmf-checkpoint, iso-42001-audit-prep, hitl-compliance-gate, ai-transparency-writer, pii-exposure-checker, incident-response-logger) are derived directly from primary regulatory and standards text:

- **EU AI Act** — Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 (Articles 6, 9, 10, 13, 14, 62)
- **NIST AI RMF 1.0** — NIST AI 100-1, January 2023 (Govern / Map / Measure / Manage functions)
- **ISO/IEC 42001:2023** — AI Management System standard (§4, §6, §8, §9, §10)
- **GDPR** — Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (Articles 5, 25, 35)
- **NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 2** — Computer Security Incident Handling Guide (§3.1–§3.4)

Each skill maps named articles, sections, and clauses from these sources. Skills are practitioner-authored and production-tested through operational use on the Quirgs platform. Human-in-the-loop review is applied at every authoring stage.

Not audited by a third-party compliance body. Not reviewed by legal counsel. These are governance scaffolding tools — not legal advice and not a substitute for qualified professional judgment. Every skill output carries this disclaimer explicitly.

Machine-readable provenance (primary sources, article mapping, limitations): [/.well-known/provenance.json](https://quirgs.com/.well-known/provenance.json)

## Governance & trust

- [Transparency](https://quirgs.com/transparency/): How Quirgs operates, what the skills are and are not, and the educational-use disclaimer.
- [Security](https://quirgs.com/security/): Security posture and contact. See also /.well-known/security.txt (RFC 9116).
- [Privacy](https://quirgs.com/privacy/): No personal-data collection; analytics-only posture.
- [Terms](https://quirgs.com/terms/): Terms of use.

## Optional

- [Agent catalog (ARD)](https://quirgs.com/.well-known/ai-catalog.json): Machine-readable Agentic Resource Discovery catalog of installable skills.
- [Sitemap](https://quirgs.com/sitemap-index.xml): Full URL index.
